algorithms.title: Suspected Preeclampsia/Eclampsia Field Approach
algorithms.description: Sequence for assessing and managing a pregnant patient with suspected preeclampsia or an active eclamptic seizure.
steps: Identify hypertension in a pregnant patient (typically past 20 weeks gestation).
Screen for warning signs: proteinuria history, severe/persistent headache, visual disturbances, right upper quadrant/epigastric pain, sudden significant swelling.
If preeclampsia is suspected: position in left lateral tilt or with manual uterine displacement, minimize stimulation, monitor blood pressure closely.
If a new seizure or coma occurs in this patient: treat as eclampsia — apply standard seizure airway and injury-prevention precautions.
Once the seizure allows, position in left lateral tilt; continue to minimize stimulation.
Transport rapidly to an obstetric-capable facility; magnesium sulfate administration for eclampsia follows local ALS protocol.
Continue monitoring for repeat seizure and worsening signs throughout transport.
